---
name: "\U0001F4DA Documentation Issue"
about: Report issues or suggest improvements to documentation, README, guides, API
  docs, etc.
title: ''
labels: documentation
assignees: ''
type: Task

---

Thanks for helping improve our documentation!
Please provide as much detail as possible so we can address it efficiently.
Feel free to remove sections that don't apply.

Which documentation is affected?

- Location / Page / File: (e.g. README.md, docs/getting-started.md, API reference /user endpoint, wiki page "Installation", website URL https://example.com/docs/..., etc.)
- Version / Branch: (e.g. latest / v2.4.0 / main branch as of 2026-03-01 / specific commit hash)

Describe the issue or suggestion

A clear and concise description of what's wrong or could be better.  
Examples: typo/grammar error, outdated/incorrect information, missing explanation/step, unclear wording, better examples needed, accessibility issue, suggestion for new section/tutorial/diagram.

Current state (what's there now)

Paste or describe the problematic text/section here (you can copy-paste directly or link to the line(s) on GitHub if applicable, e.g. https://github.com/username/repo/blob/main/docs/guide.md#L42-L58).

Suggested change / improvement

Describe what you think should be there instead. Feel free to propose rewritten text, new structure, or additional content.

Why is this important? (optional but helpful)

- Who does this affect? (new users, advanced users, specific personas)
- Impact: confusing for beginners / leads to support questions / blocks adoption / etc.

Screenshots / Examples (optional but very helpful)

Attach screenshots of the rendered docs, before/after mocks, confusing UI, etc. if relevant.

Additional context

- Related issues / PRs:
- Environment where you noticed it (browser, device, dark/light mode if relevant):
- Any workarounds you're using:
- Links to similar/better docs in other projects:

---

Tip for maintainers / triage team:
- Common labels to add after review: good first issue, docs, help wanted, low-effort, accessibility
- If it's a quick typo/fix → consider good first issue
- If larger rewrite needed → may pair with enhancement
